Use 56px tabular-numeral figures in Source Serif 4 or JetBrains Mono — the same treatment as the 'since you hired Alex' hero in the AgentPanel — to make numbers feel like a briefing, not a dashboard.
applyToSwitchboard.rationale: Intercom leads with three hero metrics. Pabau confirms cost-per-booked is the medspa operator's real KPI. Mercury's typography doctrine: financial figures need their own size/weight tier to be authoritative. The cumulative framing ('since Riley launched') directly answers 'is this worth $100/mo?' — the only question that matters for retention.

applyToSwitchboard.surface: Results screen — the 'so what' interpretation layer
applyToSwitchboard.recommendation: Below each hero metric, render one plain-English sentence generated from real data: 'Riley's cost per booked appointment dropped 34% since you raised the daily budget cap on May 12.' This is the Klipfolio PowerMetrics pattern: [metric] changed [by X] [because of/after Y]. Where the cause is unknown, omit the causal clause rather than fabricating it: 'Bookings are up 18% week-over-week.' Never ship an em-dash or 'wiring up next week' in a live production metric.
applyToSwitchboard.rationale: Stripe's doctrine: 'Data tells you what happened; an insight tells you what it means and how to respond.' Klipfolio's AI narrative layer is the pattern: plain-English interpretation adjacent to the number. The audit gap here is real — the current Results screen has a worsening delta in near-invisible ink-3 gray with no interpretive sentence. This is the single highest-leverage addition to make the screen feel like a trusted colleague rather than a raw scoreboard.

applyToSwitchboard.surface: Results screen — attribution language everywhere
applyToSwitchboard.recommendation: Replace all 'generated', 'drove', or 'produced' language with 'handled', 'followed up with', 'attributed', or 'assisted'. The canonical sentence template: '[Agent] handled [N] [leads/inquiries/contacts]; [M] booked an appointment.' For ad spend: 'Riley spent $[X] on Meta ads this week; $[Y] in bookings are attributed to those campaigns via CRM match.' Never surface platform-reported ROAS as a hero metric — it over-reports. Show CRM-verified bookings first; fold platform metrics into an expandable 'Ad platform data' row.
applyToSwitchboard.rationale: Forrester 2024: 64% of marketing leaders don't trust their own measurement because of platform over-reporting. Honest attribution language ('handled N leads, M booked') is verifiable by the operator against their own calendar. The conservative framing is more persuasive, not less — it's the same technique Stripe uses ('Retool recovered $600K' in past tense, attributed to named company, not claimed as a feature outcome).

applyToSwitchboard.surface: Results screen — bad news / worsening delta treatment
applyToSwitchboard.recommendation: A declining metric delta needs a distinct visual treatment at sufficient contrast — not red (alarming) and not ink-3 gray (~3.6:1, current, near-invisible). The Mercury model: desaturated amber/cool-slate at 4.5:1 minimum, paired with a directional glyph (▼) and the interpretive sentence. The key invariant from the AgentPanel gold standard: '0 is shown dimmed-but-present' and 'error never reads as all clear.' A worsening cost-per-booked must be legible at a glance, not require the operator to squint at a percent sign they weren't expecting.
applyToSwitchboard.rationale: Current audit finding: deltaNeg = ink-3 ~3.6:1, the lowest-contrast element on the Results page. Mercury's desaturated semantic color system proves you can make bad news readable without making it alarming. The Nielsen Norman Group principle: 'hiding errors or presenting probabilistic outputs as definitive destroys trust faster than the errors themselves.'

applyToSwitchboard.surface: Results screen — sparklines and trend visualization
applyToSwitchboard.recommendation: Every hero metric gets an inline 7-or-14-day sparkline (Tufte's 'word-sized graphic') immediately to the right of the number. No axes, no labels, no legend — just the shape of the trend. Use operator-amber for upward trend, cool-slate for downward, neutral gray for flat. The sparkline communicates direction in one glance without requiring navigation to a chart view. Keep the data window visible as a label: '7d' or '30d' in tertiary type below the sparkline.
applyToSwitchboard.rationale: Tufte: sparklines 'reduce recency bias' by showing the run, not just today. The current Results screen has no sparklines. Domo's sparkline guide confirms: use when you need to show trend direction for many metrics in compact space — exactly the Results card layout. The 40/30/20/10 space allocation principle: 40% of card area to hero number, 30% to secondary KPIs, 20% to trend context (sparkline), 10% to label/filter.

applyToSwitchboard.surface: Results screen — agent-attributed work log (the 'what we actually did' section)
applyToSwitchboard.recommendation: Below the metrics panel, ship a readable 'What [Agent] did this week' log: a list of 5-10 specific actions in natural language, each with an outcome. 'Sent follow-up to Kezia M. after no response for 48 hours → booked Fri 2pm. Responded to 3 Instagram DMs (10pm) → 1 consultation scheduled.' This is the Smashing Magazine agentic AI pattern: Action Audit as chronological timeline of agent-initiated actions with clear status and time-limited undo. For operators who distrust 'Riley generated $X', being able to read the actual messages/actions is the proof-of-work that makes the number credible.
applyToSwitchboard.rationale: Smashing Magazine: 'A persistent, easy-to-read Action Audit log is the ultimate safety net that dramatically lowers the perceived risk of granting autonomy.' Intercom's Fin dashboard narrative structure follows: top-line → involvement patterns → resolution quality — the log is the 'involvement patterns' layer. The Switchboard WorkTrace is the data source; the gap is the readable UI surface over it.

applyToSwitchboard.surface: Results screen — comparison / period selector
applyToSwitchboard.recommendation: Default the time selector to 'last 7 days' but surface a 'since [Agent] joined' option as a first-class button, not a dropdown. The 'since hired' view shows the cumulative number that answers 'is this worth it?' — it should be reachable in one tap, not buried in a date-picker. Every metric panel must show its time window as an explicit label ('this week', 'since May 3', '30-day rolling') so mixed-window displays are never ambiguous.
applyToSwitchboard.rationale: Mercury Insights: 'compare timeframes' as the primary interaction model. The Switchboard AgentPanel already ships a 'since you hired Alex' hero — but only in the panel sheet. Bringing that framing to the Results screen primary view answers the retention question on the screen operators see most.

applyToSwitchboard.surface: Results screen — skeleton / loading state
applyToSwitchboard.recommendation: Results needs a layout-matched skeleton: same card proportions and text-block positions as the loaded state, using --bg-subtle pulsing placeholders. Gate the skeleton correctly using the keys-pending-safe rule (!data && !error, not isLoading) so the screen never flashes a false-empty 'no results' state while data is still loading. Add app/(auth)/results/loading.tsx as a route-level shell to ensure the skeleton paints within the first frame after navigation.
applyToSwitchboard.rationale: Current audit finding: no loading.tsx for any authed route; Inbox already demonstrates the !data && !error fix (mira-desk-page.tsx:34). The perceived-performance gap on Results is identical — a blank screen on load reads as 'broken' to an operator checking their ROI on a mobile device between appointments.

applyToSwitchboard.surface: Results screen — typography / number rendering
applyToSwitchboard.recommendation: All financial figures on the Results screen must use a font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums CSS declaration (fixing the current no-op) so KPI numbers don't jitter when they update. The hero tier (cost-per-booked, attributed revenue, bookings count) should render in JetBrains Mono or Source Serif 4 at a larger size (40-56px) with -0.5px tracking, matching Mercury's treatment of account balances as a distinct typographic tier. Secondary metrics (delta percentages, sparkline values) stay in Inter at the standard scale but must use tabular figures.
applyToSwitchboard.rationale: Mercury's typography doctrine: 'account balances are not body text' — they need 500 weight, -0.5px tracking, and a size tier that makes them instantly scannable. Current audit: data-tabular is a no-op in the app because the CSS property is applied but the font doesn't have the feature loaded. This requires explicitly loading the tabular-nums variant of Inter or switching the metric face to JetBrains Mono (already in the design system) for financial figures.

applyToSwitchboard.surface: Results screen — no-activity and quiet-period honest states
applyToSwitchboard.recommendation: When an agent ran no campaigns (budget cap hit, paused, Meta approval pending), show a specific, named empty state — not a blank card or a zero with no context. 'Riley's Meta ads are paused — your daily budget cap of $50 was reached on Tuesday. Tap to adjust.' This is the Smashing Magazine 'Escalation Pathways' pattern applied to Results: rather than guessing, name the constraint and offer the next action. Never collapse 'agent paused' into the same visual as 'agent ran but got zero results' — these are operationally different and the operator needs to know which it is.
applyToSwitchboard.rationale: The AgentPanel 'three-states-never-collapse' invariant (loading/error/empty each have their own branch, error never reads as 0) is the gold standard from the current codebase. The Results screen needs the same discipline. A quiet week with no ad spend should look different from a week where spend ran but got no bookings — both are honest, both are useful, neither should be a blank card.
